chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ أَوْصَى بِعِتْقٍ أَوْ صَدَقَةٍ أَوْ حَجٍّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – One who bequeaths with the libration of a slave, or charity, or Hajj

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/30)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/30)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى بِثَلَاثِينَ دِينَاراً يُعْتَقُ بِهَا رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا فَلَمْ يُوجَدْ بِذَلِكَ قَالَ يُشْتَرَى مِنَ النَّاسِ فَيُعْتَقُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Ali Bin Abu Hama who said,

‘I asked Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> about a man who bequeathed with thirty Dinars to emancipate (a slave) with it, a man from our companions. So that could not be found. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Buy (a slave) from the people, so emancipate him’.
en.sarwar: 9. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Ali ibn abu Hamzah who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who has made a will about thirty dinars to set free a man from our people but it cannot be done with that much. (The Imam) said, ‘A slave then must be bought from other people for this purpose.’”
